complete_backup.title: Complete Backup
complete_backup.description_prefix: Download a complete backup containing database, notes and attachments for
complete_backup.use_case_restore_text: Import this backup to recover your data in case of loss
complete_backup.use_case_offline_text_suffix: to browse your notes without Poznote

structured_export.title: Structured Export
structured_export.description: Export your notes organized in folders that match your Poznote folder structure. Each note will be saved in a directory that corresponds to its folder in Poznote, preserving the full hierarchy. Markdown files include YAML frontmatter with metadata.
structured_export.warning: Note: This export type is not designed for a perfect Poznote backup.

restore_from_backup.title: Do you want to restore from a Poznote ZIP backup file?
restore_from_backup.description: Restore your Poznote database from a complete backup

standard_restore.title: Standard Restore
standard_restore.description: Choose a complete backup ZIP file.

direct_copy_restore.title: Restore when standard restore doesn't work (timeout if zip file is too large)
direct_copy_restore.step1: 1. Copy the file to the container:

individual_notes.title: Do you want to import HTML, Markdown, text files or ZIP archives?
individual_notes.description: Import individual notes (HTML, Markdown or ZIP) to a specific workspace and folder
individual_notes.frontmatter_warning: If an MD note contains a folder key in a front matter, this value will override the one selected below. You must therefore ensure first that the folder already exists, otherwise the note will be created at the root of the workspace.
individual_notes.zip_folders_description: If your ZIP contains folders, they will be automatically created as folders in Poznote, preserving their hierarchy (subfolders included).
individual_notes.files_info: Multiple files (max {{maxIndividualFiles}}) or single ZIP file (max {{maxZipFiles}} files). These limits can be changed,
individual_notes.description_html:
Import individual HTML, Markdown, text or JSON files, or a ZIP archive containing multiple files.

Title will be automatically created from filename (without extension), unless a title key is defined in a front matter for MD files.

Tasklists: .json files containing tasks arrays and .md files with checkbox syntax (- [ ] or - [x]) are automatically imported as tasklists.
individual_notes.helper: You can select multiple files at once (Maximum 50 files). Supported formats: .html, .md, .markdown, .txt, .json

import_confirm.title: Are you sure?
import_confirm.body: This will replace note titles, tags, search index and metadata, but not actual note content. This action cannot be undone.

complete_restore.title: Complete restore?
complete_restore.warning_large_html:
Warning: This file is {{size}}MB. Standard upload might be slow or fail for large files.

This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.
complete_restore.warning_html: Warning: This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.
complete_restore.body_html: This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.

import_notes.title: Import notes?
import_notes.body: This will import all HTML files from the ZIP. Files will be added to your existing notes collection.

import_attachments.title: Import attachments?
import_attachments.body: This will import all files from the ZIP to attachments folder. Files will be added to your existing attachments.

complete_restore_direct_copy.title: Complete restore (Direct copy)?
complete_restore_direct_copy.body_html: This will replace your database, restore all notes and attachments for all workspaces.

import_individual_notes.title: Import individual notes?
import_individual_notes.body: This will import notes to Poznote workspace.

notifications.activated_message: This folder is now in Kanban mode. Click its icon to open the board.

list.hint: Enable Kanban view on a folder to see it here.

search.placeholder_notes: Search for one or more words...
search.placeholder_tags: Search for one or more tags...

validation.select_type: Please select at least one search option (Notes or Tags)
validation.single_letter_warning: Searching with single-letter words can return too many results. Try using longer words for a more precise search.

git_sync.title: {{provider}} Sync
git_sync.description: Synchronize your notes and attachments to GitHub or Forgejo. Files are stored as flat files with a metadata.json that preserves tags, folders, dates and attachments.
git_sync.warning:
Do not rely on this feature as your sole backup.
Regularly export your data using the backup feature in Settings.
git_sync.confirm_push: Push all notes and attachments to {{provider}}? Existing files will be updated and files no longer in Poznote will be removed from the repository.
git_sync.confirm_pull: Pull all notes and attachments from {{provider}}? Local data will be overwritten with the remote version. Notes not found on {{provider}} will be moved to trash.
git_sync.confirm_auto_pull: A new session started. Do you want to pull changes from {{provider}}?
git_sync.confirm_auto_pull_warning:
A new session started. Do you want to pull changes from {{provider}}?

Local notes not found on {{provider}} will be moved to trash.
git_sync.auto_pull_no_changes: Git pull complete. No changes found.

config.title: Configuration
config.hint: {{provider}} sync is disabled. An administrator should set POZNOTE_GIT_SYNC_ENABLED=true in your .env file to enable it.
config.hint_enable: Git sync is disabled. An administrator should set POZNOTE_GIT_SYNC_ENABLED=true in your environment variables to enable it.

auto_sync.title: Automatic Synchronization
auto_sync.push_description: When leaving a note (switching notes or closing the tab), its latest version is automatically pushed to the Git repository, if it was modified during the session.
auto_sync.pull_description: Once per session, asks whether to pull from the Git repository. Updates existing notes, imports new ones, and moves to trash any local notes not found on the remote.

push.title: Push to {{provider}}
push.description: Exports all your notes and attachments to {{provider}} as flat files. Updates existing files and removes files that no longer exist in Poznote.

pull.title: Pull from {{provider}}
pull.description: Imports all notes and attachments from {{provider}}. Overwrites local content and moves to trash any notes not present in the repository.

messages.push_success: Successfully pushed {{count}} notes, deleted {{deleted}} files ({{errors}} errors)
messages.pull_success: Successfully pulled {{pulled}} new notes, updated {{updated}} existing notes, deleted {{deleted}} notes ({{errors}} errors)

limitations.title: Synchronization Limitations
limitations.embedded_note: Embedded images are preserved in HTML but not displayed on {{provider}}

not_configured.title: {{provider}} Sync Not Configured
not_configured.description: To use {{provider}} synchronization, you need to configure your {{provider}} credentials in the .env file.
not_configured.step1: Create a {{provider}} Personal Access Token at with 'repo' scope
not_configured.step2: Create a {{provider}} repository (public or private) for your notes
not_configured.step3: Add the configuration to your .env file
not_configured.step4: Restart the application for changes to take effect

convert_images.title: Base64 Image Converter
convert_images.description: Finds inline base64-encoded images stored in notes and extracts them as proper attachment files, reducing database size and improving performance. Useful after restoring a backup that was created when notes still contained base64-encoded images.
convert_images.scan_desc: Analyse all notes across every user account and report how many inline base64 images were found.
convert_images.convert_desc: Extract every base64 image and save it as a file on disk. Notes are updated to reference the new attachment URL.
convert_images.notice_convert_clean: No base64 images found. Nothing to convert.
convert_images.notice_scan_clean: No base64 images found across all users. Nothing to do.
convert_images.notice_scan_found: {{count}} inline image(s) found in {{notes}} note(s). Use Step 2 to convert them.

orphan_scanner.title: Orphan attachments scanner
orphan_scanner.description: This tool scans your attachment folders and identifies files that are no longer referenced in any of your notes (including inline images, which are stored as attachments).
orphan_scanner.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to permanently delete these files?

admin.admin_id_1_locked: Administrator role cannot be removed from user ID 1
admin.email_usage_note: Email addresses are only used for OIDC authentication if configured. Poznote does not send any emails.
admin.admin_note: Use the Admin checkbox to grant or revoke administrator privileges for another user.

confirm_admin.message: Are you sure you want to grant administrator privileges to "{{username}}" ?
confirm_admin.privileges_title: The administrator will be able to:
confirm_admin.privilege_notes: Export notes from any user into a ZIP file
confirm_admin.privilege_passwords: Change passwords of all other users

password_management.reset_help: Remove the custom password and fall back to the environment variable default for this user.
password_management.reset_confirm: Reset password for "{{username}}" to the environment variable default?
password_management.reset_success: Password reset to the default value found in the environment variables.
password_management.status_custom_detail: This user uses a custom password.
password_management.status_default_detail: This user uses the default password found in the environment variables.

errors.cannot_change_self: You cannot change your own status/role
